projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: Roxanne slept only 1.86h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

The sustained high device count (17-20) paired with zero camera motion across a 1.5-hour evening window strongly indicates a sensor fault (either the presence tracker is overcounting or the cameras are offline) rather than normal household activity, which compromises reliable security and occupancy monitoring.

The persistent 16-20 device count combined with zero camera motion during typical after-school hours confirms the presence sensor is tracking background IoT devices rather than occupancy, and the lack of camera activity suggests motion detection may be disabled or the cameras are offline, warranting a quick system check.

The persistent 16-19 device count paired with zero camera motion confirms the presence sensor is tracking background IoT/network devices rather than actual household members, which is a common network artifact and not a household anomaly.

The persistent 16-19 connected devices paired with zero camera motion throughout the Monday afternoon strongly suggests the home is likely unoccupied, with the high count driven by stationary IoT electronics rather than human presence. This aligns with typical school-day schedules and confirms the presence metric is being skewed by always-on devices, requiring no immediate action.

The persistent 18-20 connected devices combined with zero camera motion over four hours strongly indicates the house is currently unoccupied, with phones, tablets, and smart home hubs left connected to Wi-Fi. This aligns with a typical Monday afternoon schedule, confirming no one is actively moving through the monitored areas.

The persistent 18-19 connected mobile devices paired with zero camera motion on a Monday afternoon strongly suggests a network presence detection glitch or an unoccupied home with lingering connections, which may cause occupancy-based automations to malfunction.

The combination of ~18-21 connected devices and zero camera motion on a Monday afternoon strongly indicates the entire household is home and sedentary, deviating from the typical work/school schedule. This confirms the house is occupied but quiet, which is valuable for security context and routine tracking.

The consistent 19-21 connected devices paired with zero camera motion on a Monday morning suggests the household is still fully present, possibly with guests or on a remote/sick day, and the camera system should be verified for proper functionality.

The persistent 17-21 connected mobile devices overnight and throughout Monday morning, paired with zero camera or audio activity, strongly indicates unattended devices left connected in a specific zone or a network tracking artifact rather than active occupancy. This warrants a quick check to rule out unsecured guest access or misconfigured device profiles.
